mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates ongoing labor market tightening and wage-driven consumption expansion, which increases aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159001 thousands as of Sep 2017
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observed months.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that elevates the equity discount rate and compresses valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in industrial production and retail sales validates a demand loop where factory output is absorbed by consumer spending, amplifying earnings optimism and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than 0.03 with 60-day SPX returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already below 4.5%, further declines signal a tight labor market that threatens margin compression via wage inflation and faster Fed tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE below 4.5% fail to produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI index exceeding 0.25% accelerates expectations of monetary policy tightening, raising real discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.25% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and payroll employment confirms broad-based consumer demand that is not driven by credit alone, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when both series rise together.
failure_condition: Co-movement of RSAFS and PAYEMS does not predict positive 20-day SPX returns with a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals rising capacity utilization and manufacturing earnings leverage, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice near-term earnings.
failure_condition: Weeks following above-median month-over-month INDPRO changes do not show positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% confirms durable domestic demand and reduces recession probability, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as risk premia compress.
failure_condition: Quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases.
